docs.title: pyhmfd
docs.text: pyhmfd is a Python package for reading data from five major demographic databases directly into pandas.DataFrame objects — ready for analysis, plotting, or modelling.

docs.title: Supported databases
docs.text: Database Short name Authentication Human Mortality Database HMD account required Human Fertility Database HFD account required Human Fertility Collection HFC none Japanese Mortality Database JMD none Canadian Historical Mortality Database CHMD none

docs.title: Quick example
docs.text:
import pyhmfd

# Single country
df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web("AUS", "Mx_1x1")

# Multiple countries in parallel
df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web_multi(["AUS", "USA", "SWE"], "Mx_1x1", workers=4)
print(df["country"].unique())  # ['AUS', 'SWE', 'USA']

docs.title: Credits
docs.text:
This package is a Python port of the R package HMDHFDplus by Tim Riffe, Jose Manuel Aburto, and contributors:

Riffe T, Aburto JM, et al. (2023). HMDHFDplus: Read Human Mortality Database and Human Fertility Database Data from the Web. R package. https://github.com/timriffe/HMDHFDplus

Licensed under GPL-2.0.

docs.title: pyhmfd.hmd.read_hmd(filepath, fixup=True)
docs.text: Read a locally saved HMD text file into a DataFrame.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: filepath: Path to the HMD .txt file (e.g. Mx_1x1.txt). fixup: If True, run :func:~pyhmfd.parsers.hmd_parse to coerce column types and add the OpenInterval indicator column.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame

docs.title: pyhmfd.hmd.read_hmd_web(country, item, username=None, password=None, fixup=True)
docs.text: Download one data item for one country from the HMD.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: country: HMD country short code (e.g. "USA", "FRATNP"). Use :func:get_hmd_countries to list valid codes. item: Data item name (e.g. "Mx_1x1", "Deaths_1x1"). Use :func:get_hmd_items to list available items per country. username: HMD account e-mail. Resolved from HMD_USER env var if omitted. password: HMD account password. Resolved from HMD_PASSWORD env var or keyring if omitted. fixup: If True, coerce column types and add OpenInterval column.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: import pyhmfd df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web("USA", "Mx_1x1")

docs.title: pyhmfd.multi.read_hmd_web_multi(countries, item, username=None, password=None, fixup=True, workers=4, on_error='warn')
docs.text: Download one HMD data item for multiple countries in parallel.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: countries: List of HMD country short codes, or "all" to download every available country. Use :func:~pyhmfd.get_hmd_countries to list valid codes. item: Data item name (e.g. "Mx_1x1", "Deaths_1x1"). username: HMD account e-mail. Resolved from HMD_USER env var if omitted. password: HMD account password. Resolved from HMD_PASSWORD env var or keyring if omitted. fixup: If True, coerce column types and add OpenInterval column. workers: Number of parallel download threads. on_error: "warn" (default) — skip failing countries and emit a warning. "raise" — raise the first error encountered.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame Concatenated data with a leading country column.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: import pyhmfd df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web_multi(["AUS", "USA", "SWE"], "Mx_1x1") df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web_multi("all", "Deaths_1x1", workers=8)

docs.title: pyhmfd.hmd.get_hmd_countries()
docs.text: Return all HMD country and sub-population short codes.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: list[str] e.g. ['AUS', 'AUT', ..., 'USA']

docs.title: pyhmfd.hmd.get_hmd_items(country)
docs.text: List all data items available for a given HMD country.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: country: HMD country short code.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame Columns: item, description, url.

docs.title: pyhmfd.hfd.read_hfd(filepath, fixup=True, item=None)
docs.text: Read a locally saved HFD text file into a DataFrame.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: filepath: Path to the HFD .txt file. fixup: If True, run :func:~pyhmfd.parsers.hfd_parse to coerce column types and add the OpenInterval indicator column. item: Data product code (e.g. "mabRR"). Used by the parser to determine column types when fixup=True.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame

docs.title: pyhmfd.hfd.read_hfd_web(country, item, username=None, password=None, fixup=True)
docs.text: Download one data item for one country from the HFD.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: country: HFD country short code (e.g. "USA", "FRATNP"). Use :func:get_hfd_countries to list valid codes. item: Data product code (e.g. "asfrRR", "mabRR"). Use :func:get_hfd_items to list available items per country. username: HFD account e-mail. Resolved from HFD_USER env var if omitted. password: HFD account password. Resolved from HFD_PASSWORD env var or keyring if omitted. fixup: If True, coerce column types and add OpenInterval column.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: import pyhmfd df = pyhmfd.read_hfd_web("USA", "asfrRR")

docs.title: pyhmfd.multi.read_hfd_web_multi(countries, item, username=None, password=None, fixup=True, workers=4, on_error='warn')
docs.text: Download one HFD data item for multiple countries in parallel.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: countries: List of HFD country short codes, or "all" to download every available country. Use :func:~pyhmfd.get_hfd_countries to list valid codes. item: Data product code (e.g. "asfrRR", "mabRR"). username: HFD account e-mail. Resolved from HFD_USER env var if omitted. password: HFD account password. Resolved from HFD_PASSWORD env var or keyring if omitted. fixup: If True, coerce column types and add OpenInterval column. workers: Number of parallel download threads. on_error: "warn" (default) — skip failing countries and emit a warning. "raise" — raise the first error encountered.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame Concatenated data with a leading country column.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: import pyhmfd df = pyhmfd.read_hfd_web_multi(["USA", "SWE", "JPN"], "asfrRR") df = pyhmfd.read_hfd_web_multi("all", "asfrRR", workers=6)

docs.title: pyhmfd.hfd.get_hfd_countries()
docs.text: Return a DataFrame of all HFD countries.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame Columns: country (full name), code (short code).

docs.title: pyhmfd.hfd.get_hfd_items(country)
docs.text: List all data items available for a given HFD country.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: country: HFD country short code.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame Columns: item, description, url.

docs.title: pyhmfd.hfd.get_hfd_date(country)
docs.text: Return the last-update date for a given HFD country.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: country: HFD country short code.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: str Eight-digit date string in 'YYYYMMDD' format.

docs.title: pyhmfd.jmd.read_jmd_web(pref_id='00', item='Deaths_5x5', fixup=True)
docs.text:
Download data for a Japanese prefecture from the JMD.

No authentication is required.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: pref_id: Two-digit prefecture code ('00' = all Japan, '01' = Hokkaido, …, '47' = Okinawa). Use :func:~pyhmfd.hmd.get_jmd_prefectures to list all codes. item: Data item name (e.g. 'Deaths_5x5', 'Mx_1x1'). fixup: If True, coerce column types and add OpenInterval column.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: import pyhmfd df = pyhmfd.read_jmd_web("01", "Deaths_1x1")

docs.title: pyhmfd.hmd.get_jmd_prefectures()
docs.text:
Return a mapping of Japanese prefecture name → 2-digit code.

Codes run from '00' (all Japan) to '47' (Okinawa).

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: dict[str, str]

docs.title: pyhmfd.chmd.read_chmd_web(prov_id='can', item='Deaths_1x1', fixup=True)
docs.text:
Download data for a Canadian province from the CHMD.

No authentication is required.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: prov_id: Province short code ('can' = all Canada, 'qc' = Quebec, …). Use :func:~pyhmfd.hmd.get_chmd_provinces to list all codes. item: Data item name (e.g. 'Deaths_1x1', 'Mx_1x1'). fixup: If True, coerce column types and add OpenInterval column.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: import pyhmfd df = pyhmfd.read_chmd_web("que", "Mx_1x1")

docs.title: pyhmfd.hmd.get_chmd_provinces()
docs.text: Return Canadian province/territory codes for the CHMD.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: list[str] e.g. ['alb', 'bco', 'can', ...]

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: CHMD uses its own 3-letter codes, distinct from standard ISO abbreviations: can (Canada), que (Quebec), ont (Ontario), bco (British Columbia), alb (Alberta), man (Manitoba), nbr (New Brunswick), nsc (Nova Scotia), nfl (Newfoundland), pei (PEI), sas (Saskatchewan), nwt (Northwest Territories), yuk (Yukon).

docs.title: pyhmfd.hfc.read_hfc_web(country, item, fixup=True)
docs.text:
Download a data item for a country from the HFC.

No authentication is required.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: country: HFC population short code. Use :func:get_hfc_countries to list available codes. item: Data item identifier (e.g. 'ASFRstand'). fixup: If True, coerce column types and add OpenInterval column.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pandas.DataFrame

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: import pyhmfd df = pyhmfd.read_hfc_web("RUS", "ASFRstand")

docs.title: pyhmfd.hfc.get_hfc_countries(names=False)
docs.text: Return HFC population codes (and optionally full names).

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: names: If False (default), return a sorted list of short codes. If True, return a DataFrame with columns country and code.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: list[str] or pandas.DataFrame

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install pyhmfd

Or from source:

git clone https://github.com/filipeclduarte/pyhmfd.git
cd pyhmfd
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Credentials
docs.text:
The HMD and HFD require a free account. Register at:

  • HMD: mortality.org
  • HFD: humanfertility.org

docs.title: Setting credentials
docs.text:
Environment variables (recommended for scripts and CI):

export HMD_USER="your@email.com"
export HMD_PASSWORD="yourpassword"
export HFD_USER="your@email.com"
export HFD_PASSWORD="yourpassword"

Pass directly to the function:

df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web("AUS", "Mx_1x1", username="your@email.com", password="yourpassword")

Interactive prompt:

If no credentials are found, the package prompts for them in the terminal. Credentials entered interactively are stored in the system keyring so you won't be asked again.

Tip

For automated workflows (CI/CD, notebooks), always use environment variables to avoid interactive prompts.

docs.title: Human Fertility Database (HFD)
docs.text: The Human Fertility Database provides age-specific fertility rates and related indicators for about 40 countries. An account is required.

docs.title: Reading data from the web
docs.text:
import pyhmfd

# Age-specific fertility rates, period (rectangles)
df = pyhmfd.read_hfd_web("USA", "asfrRR")

# Mean age at birth
df = pyhmfd.read_hfd_web("SWE", "mabRR")

# Total fertility rate
df = pyhmfd.read_hfd_web("JPN", "tfrRR")

docs.title: Reading a local file
docs.text: df = pyhmfd.read_hfd("/path/to/asfrRR.txt", item="asfrRR")

docs.title: Listing countries and items
docs.text:
# All available countries
countries = pyhmfd.get_hfd_countries()
print(countries)
#       country  code
# 0      Austria   AUT
# 1      Belarus   BLR
# ...

# Items available for a specific country
items = pyhmfd.get_hfd_items("USA")
print(items)

# Date of last update for a country
print(pyhmfd.get_hfd_date("USA"))  # '20260323'

docs.title: Common data items
docs.text:
Item Description asfrRR Age-specific fertility rates, period (rectangles) asfrTR Age-specific fertility rates, period (triangles) asfrVH Age-specific fertility rates, cohort tfrRR Total fertility rate, period mabRR Mean age at birth, period birthsRR Live births, period exposRR Female population exposure, period

Country availability

Australia is not in the HFD. Use get_hfd_countries() to see the full list of available countries.

docs.title: Human Mortality Database (HMD)
docs.text: The Human Mortality Database provides detailed mortality and population data for over 40 countries. An account is required.

docs.title: Reading data from the web
docs.text:
import pyhmfd

# Death rates (age × year, 1×1)
df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web("AUS", "Mx_1x1")

# Deaths by Lexis triangle
df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web("USA", "Deaths_lexis")

# Life tables
df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web("SWE", "fltper_1x1")

docs.title: Reading a local file
docs.text:
If you have already downloaded a file from the HMD website:

df = pyhmfd.read_hmd("/path/to/Mx_1x1.txt")

docs.title: Listing countries and items
docs.text:
# All available country codes
countries = pyhmfd.get_hmd_countries()
print(countries)  # ['AUS', 'AUT', 'BEL', ..., 'USA']

# Items available for a specific country
items = pyhmfd.get_hmd_items("AUS")
print(items)
#           item                description
# 0      Mx_1x1      Death rates (period 1x1)
# 1      Mx_1x5      Death rates (period 1x5)
# ...

docs.title: Common data items
docs.text:
Item Description Mx_1x1 Age-specific death rates, period 1×1 Deaths_1x1 Deaths, period 1×1 Exposures_1x1 Population exposures, period 1×1 fltper_1x1 Period life tables, females mltper_1x1 Period life tables, males bltper_1x1 Period life tables, both sexes Population Population counts

Sub-populations

Some countries have sub-population codes, e.g. FRATNP (France, total), FRACNP (France, civilian). Use get_hmd_countries() to see all codes.

docs.title: Multi-country Download
docs.text: pyhmfd can download data for multiple countries in a single call. A single authenticated session is shared across all requests, and downloads run in parallel using a thread pool — significantly faster than looping.

docs.title: Basic usage
docs.text:
import pyhmfd

# HMD — three countries at once
df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web_multi(
    countries=["AUS", "USA", "SWE"],
    item="Mx_1x1",
)
print(df["country"].unique())  # ['AUS', 'SWE', 'USA']
print(df.shape)                # (rows for all three combined, columns)

# HFD — multiple countries
df = pyhmfd.read_hfd_web_multi(
    countries=["USA", "SWE", "JPN"],
    item="asfrRR",
)

docs.title: Download all countries
docs.text:
Pass countries="all" to automatically fetch the full list and download every available country:

# All HMD countries
df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web_multi(countries="all", item="Mx_1x1")

# All HFD countries
df = pyhmfd.read_hfd_web_multi(countries="all", item="asfrRR")

docs.title: Controlling parallelism
docs.text:
The workers parameter controls the number of parallel download threads (default: 4). Increase it for faster bulk downloads:

df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web_multi(
    countries="all",
    item="Mx_1x1",
    workers=8,
)

Be considerate

Avoid setting workers too high. The HMD and HFD servers are public research infrastructure — excessive parallel requests may be rate-limited or flagged. Values between 4 and 8 are generally safe.

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text:
By default (on_error="warn"), countries that fail are skipped and a warning is emitted. The remaining countries are still downloaded.

# Default: warn and continue
df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web_multi(
    ["AUS", "USA", "INVALID"],
    "Mx_1x1",
    on_error="warn",   # default
)
# UserWarning: Failed to download 1 country/countries:
#   INVALID: HMD INVALID/Mx_1x1 failed with HTTP 404

# Strict: raise on first error
df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web_multi(
    ["AUS", "USA"],
    "Mx_1x1",
    on_error="raise",
)

docs.title: Output format
docs.text:
The result is a single pandas.DataFrame with:

  • A leading country column containing the short code.
  • All the usual columns for the requested item (Year, Age, Female, Male, Total, OpenInterval, …).
  • Sorted by country, then Year, then Age.
df = pyhmfd.read_hmd_web_multi(["AUS", "USA"], "Mx_1x1")
print(df.head())
#   country  Year  Age    Female      Male     Total  OpenInterval
# 0     AUS  1921    0  0.059987  0.076533  0.068444         False
# 1     AUS  1921    1  0.012064  0.014339  0.013225         False
# ...

docs.title: Japanese Mortality Database (JMD)
docs.text:
The JMD provides mortality data for Japan's 47 prefectures. No authentication required.

import pyhmfd

# All Japan (code "00")
df = pyhmfd.read_jmd_web("00", "Mx_1x1")

# Hokkaido (code "01")
df = pyhmfd.read_jmd_web("01", "Deaths_1x1")

# List all prefecture codes
prefectures = pyhmfd.get_jmd_prefectures()
print(prefectures)
# {'All Japan': '00', 'Hokkaido': '01', 'Aomori': '02', ...}

docs.title: Canadian Historical Mortality Database (CHMD)
docs.text:
The CHMD provides mortality data for Canadian provinces and territories. No authentication required.

# All Canada
df = pyhmfd.read_chmd_web("can", "Mx_1x1")

# Quebec
df = pyhmfd.read_chmd_web("que", "Mx_1x1")

# List all province codes
provinces = pyhmfd.get_chmd_provinces()
print(provinces)
# ['alb', 'bco', 'can', 'man', 'nbr', 'nfl', 'nsc', 'nwt', 'ont', 'pei', 'que', 'sas', 'yuk']

docs.title: CHMD province codes
docs.text: Province / Territory Code Canada (all) can Alberta alb British Columbia bco Manitoba man New Brunswick nbr Newfoundland nfl Nova Scotia nsc Northwest Territories nwt Ontario ont PEI pei Quebec que Saskatchewan sas Yukon yuk

docs.title: Human Fertility Collection (HFC)
docs.text:
The HFC collects fertility data from national statistical offices for a wide range of countries and sub-populations. No authentication required.

# Russia, age-standardised ASFR
df = pyhmfd.read_hfc_web("RUS", "ASFRstand")

# List available country codes
codes = pyhmfd.get_hfc_countries()
print(codes)  # ['ARM', 'AZE', 'BLR', ...]

# Include full country names
df_countries = pyhmfd.get_hfc_countries(names=True)
print(df_countries.head())
